remove_templates: Remove the {target} and fill the space with matching wall and flooring. Keep everything else in the kitchen unchanged.
Erase the {target} from the scene and reconstruct the surfaces behind it to match the surrounding wall and floor. Do not change anything else.
Take out the {target} and seamlessly extend the adjacent wall, floor, and trim over the gap. Leave the rest of the kitchen exactly as it is.
move_templates: Move the {target} to {destination}. Rebuild the surface where it previously stood to match the surrounding materials. Keep everything else in the kitchen unchanged.
Relocate the {target} so it sits at {destination}, matching its original style and scale. Fill the old location to match the surrounding wall and floor. Do not change anything else.
Reposition the {target} to {destination}. Keep its finish and proportions consistent with the original, and leave all other cabinets, surfaces, and appliances unchanged.
open_wall_templates: Remove {wall_description} and open the kitchen to the adjacent room. Keep all other walls, cabinets, and surfaces unchanged.
Demolish {wall_description} to create an open-plan layout. Fill the opening with what would plausibly be visible beyond. Do not change anything else.
Open up {wall_description} into a wide pass-through, keeping the ceiling line and the existing finishes on every remaining surface intact.

caveats.remove: Reliable. The model only needs to rebuild surfaces it can already see, so removals are trustworthy.
caveats.move: Approximate. Placement, scale, and perspective are estimated, not measured — this shows rough feel, not a true layout.
caveats.open_wall: Approximate. The space beyond the removed wall is invented, not the real adjacent room. Use for visual inspiration only.
